consumption: Source of truth. 1055 items. Statically imported by itemCatalog.ts.

Imports items.json. Exports ITEM_CATALOG, ITEM_CATEGORIES, ITEM_RARITIES, catalogItemToInventory(), getEquippedItemBonuses(), itemPassiveBonuses(), isArmorItem(), isShieldItem(), isWeaponItem(), itemWeaponBonus(), itemArmorAcBonus(), itemMetaParts(), formatItemCost(), itemHasPassiveBonus().
Defines CatalogItem (lines 179-193), InventoryItem (lines 150-169), Equipment (lines 195-203). CatalogItem has 13 fields; InventoryItem has 17 fields.

Equipment catalog browser (search, filter, add items to inventory). Inventory display (list items, remove items). Equipment management (equip armor/shield/weapons/bonus items from inventory). Passive bonus integration (AC, saves, spell attack, spell DC). Manual item creation. All item display logic.

Uses inventory items for: computeArmorClass(), inventoryArmorBreakdown(), inventoryWeaponToDef(), getArmorProficiencyIssue(), totalCarriedWeight(). Parses item.properties for finesse, versatile, two-handed, ammunition. Uses getEquippedItemBonuses from itemCatalog.
Console command 'add-item' creates InventoryItem with randomUUID. No catalog backing. Loot response handling (separate item system).
inventoryEntry() creates InventoryItem with slugified ID. createInitialDraft() initializes empty inventory array. characterPayload() handles inventory in character creation.
starterItems array (3 hardcoded InventoryItem objects). Exposed as ruleset.items.
Creates InventoryItem for imported inventory and weapon items. Uses node:crypto randomUUID() for IDs. Creates Equipment object for imported weapons.
Defines ImportInventoryItem (name, quantity?, weight?, notes?). Quantity is typed but never mapped to InventoryItem.
Maps import fields to ImportInventoryItem structure. Creates equipmentRows Map for equipment weight mapping.
Serializes/deserializes entire Character objects (including inventory and equipment) as JSON blobs. No item-specific logic.
characters table stores inventory and equipment as JSON in data TEXT column. No item-specific columns or tables.
Tests computeArmorClass() with legacy Equipment (armorId, shield). Does NOT test inventory-backed items or catalog items.
